All candidates have mixing issues, but candidate 30 achieves good conversion despite failing intensification targets. All candidates suffer from severe mixing limitations with Da_mass >> 1 and r_mix >> 0.20. Candidate 3 is blocked due to insufficient conversion (33% < 50% minimum). All candidates suffer from severe mixing limitations with Da_mass >> 1 and r_mix > 0.20. Candidate 6 is blocked due to insufficient conversion (44% < 50% minimum). Candidates 8 and 9 have hard-gate mixing failures but can be rescued with concentration reduction. Candidate 7 is acceptable but underperforms on intensification targets. Candidate 10 achieves only 1.2x residence time reduction versus 3.0x target, falling significantly short of intensification mandate.

safety_overall: All candidates show dual-criterion mixing failures with high Damköhler numbers and poor radial mixing. Candidate 15 performs best despite long coil length. All candidates show mixing limitations with hard gate failures for 27 and 29. Candidate 30 achieves good conversion but fails intensification targets. All candidates suffer from severe mixing limitations with Da_mass >> 1 and r_mix > 0.20, causing poor conversions. Candidate 3 has insufficient conversion below 50% minimum threshold. All candidates suffer from severe mixing limitations with Da_mass >> 1 and r_mix > 0.20, indicating inadequate micromixing for this thermal cycloaddition. Candidate 6 additionally fails minimum conversion requirements. All candidates show mixing limitations with high Damköhler numbers, but candidate 7 offers the best balance of conversion and manageable mixing issues. Candidate 10 shows acceptable safety parameters with low pressure drop and appropriate BPR setting. The long coil length requires practical consideration but poses no safety risk.
